title: Downtime, Projects, Recovery, Crafting, and Long-Task Doctrine
not_final_schema_notice: Record shapes are doctrine-facing and conversion-facing controls only; runtime Gate B or later schema doctrine may replace or formalize them.
project_definition: Any goal requiring interval-scale commitment, requirements, progress, risk, or delayed state consequence.
progress_model: Interval Outcome States; numeric progress only when supported by owner file, source-local procedure, or later schema.
concurrency_model: Project Load, not universal Project slots.

downstream_handoffs.D18: campaign continuity, seasons, long-horizon pacing, state pruning

risk_fixes_embedded: Project families are routing categories, not fixed downtime menu actions.
Recovery does not import rest/reset assumptions.
Research does not reveal protected hidden truth automatically.
Crafting does not create unsupported powers or objects.
Salvage does not generate free rewards.
Contributors, facilities, and materials require role/function and may create burden.
Concurrent Projects require Project Load support.
D13 does not become D15 faction AI, D17 economy doctrine, or D18 campaign pacing.
Source-local donor downtime systems do not generalize automatically.
Record shapes are not final runtime schemas.
acceptance_criteria_summary: identify requirements before commitment when knowable
handle interruption, abandonment, pause, resume, archive
map donor systems by function, not label
